conditions: 6 AM—This is not a test. The Emergency Pow-Cast System has been activated following 24 hours of wild weather that has slathered the mountainside with 14 inches of fresh snow...so far. All vertically inclined citizens are being asked to gather their burliest storm-skiing gear and get out here, as wind-driven powder and low-elevation liquid are expected to blast the Tetons through the day. Mild high temperatures will tickle the 30-degree mark at the summit and approach 40 degrees down in the valley. Instead of cheeky rhyming, today I'm going to do a little reminding. Pack some extra patience in your pack, as ski patrol will be working hard to get you safely into the goods as quickly as possible. Heavy accumulations and these strong west-southwest winds are obviously a blessing, but they can also be a bit of a curse, as delays and lift closures may be necessary. Just enjoy the elevated atmospheric energy and be ready for an alpine experience that's extraordinary. -Lege
